ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic cream puff entry (FOODON_03302114). The Yuan Yang Kazu Kazu is a dual-flavour variant (Hokkaido milk cream + chocolate cream) sold in Singapore; no exact FoodOn code exists for this specific product.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers choux pastry as a category. No specific code exists for dual-flavour Hokkaido-chocolate cream puffs. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept exists for cream puff or choux pastry as a food product at this specificity level.

main_ingredients: choux pastry shell (wheat flour, butter, eggs, water)
Hokkaido milk cream (heavy cream, Hokkaido milk, sugar)

serving.natural_language_portion: One Yuan Yang Kazu Kazu cream puff weighs approximately 80 g and contains a baked choux pastry shell filled with both Hokkaido milk cream and chocolate cream.

nutrient_source.title: Cream puff, prepared from recipe — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18239); supplemented with USDA Branded and standard choux pastry composition data for dual-cream filling estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA for a dual-flavour Hokkaido-milk-and-chocolate cream puff. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy NDB 18239 (cream puff with custard filling, per 100 g) and adjusted upward for fat and sugar to reflect the richer Hokkaido heavy-cream and chocolate filling typical of Japanese-style premium cream puffs. The adaptation is conservative and represents a best-estimate composition. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Choux pastry / cream puff GI estimation based on analogous pastry products — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Yuan Yang Kazu Kazu cream puffs. GI of 65 is estimated from analogous choux-based pastry and cream-filled pastry products in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2008 International Tables (Atkinson et al.). Cream-filled choux pastry products with moderate sugar and fat content typically fall in the medium GI range (60–70). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 22.8) / 100 ≈ 14.8, rounded to 14.9. Serving carbohydrate = 28.5 g/100g × 80 g serving = 22.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (145 mg/100 g) and high saturated fat from cream filling
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit intake of this cream puff to occasional consumption, as the combined sodium and saturated fat load from the pastry shell and cream fillings may contribute to cardiovascular strain when consumed regularly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (65) combined with high sugar content (16 g/100 g) and refined carbohydrate from choux pastry shell
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this cream puff as an occasional indulgence only; the medium GI of 65 and high sugar content (approximately 12.8 g per 80 g serving) can cause a meaningful postprandial blood glucose rise, and portion control is essential.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (10.8 g/100 g) and cholesterol (118 mg/100 g) from heavy cream and egg-rich choux pastry
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this cream puff due to its high saturated fat and cholesterol content, which may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels when consumed frequently.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Yuan Yang Kazu Kazu (Hokkaido Milk and Chocolate Cream Puff) is a premium dual-flavour cream puff sold in Singapore by Kazo Premium, inspired by Japanese-style choux pastry traditions. The name 'Yuan Yang' (鸳鸯) refers to the classic Singaporean concept of pairing two contrasting flavours — here, a light, milky Hokkaido cream and a rich, bittersweet chocolate cream — within a single baked choux pastry shell. 'Kazu Kazu' is the brand's playful product name. The choux pastry shell is baked until golden and crisp on the outside, with a hollow interior that is generously filled with both fillings simultaneously, creating a layered flavour experience in each bite. The Hokkaido cream is made from high-fat Hokkaido milk and heavy cream, giving it a delicate sweetness and silky texture. The chocolate cream is dense, smooth, and intensely flavoured. Nutritionally, this cream puff is energy-dense at approximately 298 kcal per 100 g, with significant fat (18.5 g/100 g, predominantly saturated at 10.8 g/100 g from the cream fillings) and moderate carbohydrate (28.5 g/100 g) with high sugar (16 g/100 g). The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), making it a food that can cause a moderate postprandial blood glucose rise. It contains eggs, dairy, and gluten and is not suitable for vegans or those with these allergens. Clinically, it is a high-concern food for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and cardiovascular disease due to its saturated fat, cholesterol, and sugar content. It is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary item.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Yuan Yang Kazu Kazu cream puff exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional nutrition database. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cream puff data (NDB 18239) with adjustments for the richer Hokkaido-milk and chocolate cream filling typical of Japanese-style premium products. GI is estimated from analogous cream-filled choux pastry products in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 18239 (cream puff with custard filling) and adjusted to reflect the higher fat and sugar content of Hokkaido heavy cream and chocolate cream fillings. No exact HPB or regional database entry exists for this product. 2. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on cream-filled choux pastry products in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. 3. Serving size of 80 g is an estimate for one standard premium cream puff of this type; actual product weight may vary. 4. Halal status is listed as false because Kazo Premium's halal certification status could not be confirmed from approved sources at time of processing; this should be verified directly with the restaurant. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy NDB 18239 and are not independently verified for this specific product; they are reported as best-estimate values rather than confirmed measurements. No core macro is zero; all four core macros are positive and nutritionally plausible for a cream-filled choux pastry.
